message:
feat(stdlib): add mergesort + heapsort (BSD libc) (bd-du4q6)

BSD libc mergesort + heapsort: same shape as the existing qsort
shim except they return int (0 on success, -1 with errno on
failure). mergesort is STABLE (preserves input order for equal
elements); heapsort is NOT stable but uses no auxiliary storage.
Both are present in libbsd, used by mtree, sort -s, ls -t, and
any tool that needs stability without writing its own merge sort.

* core::stdlib::sort::mergesort copies elements into a Vec<Vec<u8>>
  and sorts via Rust's stable sort_by (timsort), then writes back —
  matches libbsd's contract of allocating temp storage proportional
  to the input.
* core::stdlib::sort::heapsort builds a max-heap in-place via
  index manipulation + element swaps, then repeatedly extracts.
* stdlib_abi::mergesort and stdlib_abi::heapsort thin shims with
  EINVAL on NULL compar / zero size and ENOMEM on overflow; nmemb
  < 2 is a no-op success.

13 core unit tests covering: sorted/reverse/random/all-equal
inputs at multiple sizes, single-element/empty/zero-width no-ops,
mergesort stability via (key, original_index) pairs, heapsort
100-element pseudorandom (overflow-safe in u64). 9 abi
integration tests covering errno mapping, stable-mergesort via
ABI with a #[repr(C)] pair struct + extern "C" comparator. Clippy-
clean, fmt-clean. All builds via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
